title: Selaginella articulata
intro: Selaginella articulata, also known as the articulated spike moss or Selaginella, is a type of spore-producing plant belonging to the family Selaginellaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in India and Southeast Asia, where it is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various ailments such as fever, sore throats, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Selaginella articulata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or a powder, and it may be used in various forms, including capsules, tablets, and teas, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically validated.
taxonomy: Selaginella articulata is a species of plant that belongs to the family Selaginellaceae. This family is part of the division Lycopodiophyta, which is a group of vascular plants that are commonly known as lycopods. Selaginellaceae is one of the four families that make up the order Selaginellales. The order Selaginellales is also part of the class Lycopodiopsida. The class Lycopodiopsida is a subgroup of the division Lycopodiophyta.
